overall.description: Third-person gameplay footage from Red Dead Redemption 2 showing a cowboy character walking through the historic streets of Saint Denis. The character moves from a cobblestone intersection down a narrow commercial street lined with balconies and storefronts, eventually arriving at the Lamarque Hotel.

scene_state.functional_zone: historic city intersection with tram tracks

events.description: character turns and begins walking away from camera down the street
horse head briefly blocks right side of frame as camera passes close by

agents.ego_description: cowboy character in hat and coat walking away from camera into the cobblestone street

notable_agents.action: woman in long dress crossing the street from left to right
tan horse tied to post on the sidewalk

entered.detail: red brick corner building with large painted signage reading D.B. RATHBONE
metal rails embedded in the cobblestone street surface
wooden telegraph poles with multiple cross arms and wires
white stone building with arched windows and G. DRAKE signage

segments.scene_description: The segment begins at a sunlit cobblestone intersection with tram tracks. The character turns and walks away from the camera down the street, passing a woman crossing from the left and a horse tied to a post on the right. The D.B. Rathbone building dominates the corner with its red brick facade.

scene_state.functional_zone: historic commercial street with wrought iron balconies

events.description: moving from open intersection into narrower street corridor with buildings on both sides
slight navigation around pedestrians walking in the path

agents.ego_description: continuing steadily down the street passing various storefronts and pedestrians

notable_agents.action: man in suspenders and cap standing on sidewalk observing the street

entered.detail: green painted cast iron railings on second floor of buildings
red white and blue striped pole on the sidewalk
building with signage becoming visible at the end of the street
wooden cart with plants and flowers parked on the sidewalk

segments.scene_description: The character walks down a narrow enclosed street lined with historic buildings featuring wrought iron balconies. The Lamarque Hotel becomes visible in the distance. The character passes a barber pole and a flower cart while navigating around scattered pedestrians.

events.description: character stops forward progress and turns right toward the hotel entrance
character looks up at the hotel balcony while approaching the entrance

agents.ego_description: character approaches the hotel, turns to face the building, and examines the balcony area

notable_agents.action: figure sitting on the hotel balcony railing overlooking the street
man in blue coat standing near the hotel entrance

entered.detail: shop with blue awning and G. VEILLEUX signage next to the hotel
second floor balcony with iron railings of the hotel
doorway with steps leading into the building

segments.scene_description: The character arrives at The Lamarque Hotel, turning to face the building. A figure sits on the hotel balcony above while another person stands near the entrance. The character examines the facade including the G. Veilleux storefront next door before the clip ends.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement from a wide, sunlit city intersection into the narrow, enclosed corridors of a historic commercial district. It begins at a cobblestone crossing with tram tracks, following the character as they pass a red brick building and a tethered horse. As the character moves forward, the open intersection gives way to a tighter street lined with wrought iron balconies, passing a barber pole and flower carts amidst a flow of pedestrians. The scene then shifts focus toward the distance as the Lamarque Hotel comes into view, eventually leading the camera to a stop directly in front of the hotel's facade. Finally, the view lingers on the building's entrance and the adjacent G. Veilleux storefront while observing figures perched on the balconies above.
